title: The Best of the Best: 10 Herbal Teas for Coughing Fits
intro_desc: Herbal teas for coughing fits are natural remedies that help alleviate the discomfort and irritation associated with persistent coughs. These teas are made from a blend of herbs, each with its unique properties and benefits, which work together to soothe the throat and calm the respiratory system. The best herbal teas for coughing fits include Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Verbascum thapsus, Sambucus nigra, Mentha x piperita, Lavandula angustifolia, and Rosmarinus officinalis. If left untreated, coughing fits can disrupt daily life, leading to sleep deprivation, fatigue, and reduced productivity, ultimately affecting one's overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a popular remedy for coughing fits. It works by reducing inflammation in the throat and airways. When we cough, it's often because our body is trying to get rid of irritants like dust, pollen, or a cold virus. Echinacea purpurea tea contains compounds that help calm down the immune system's response to these irritants, which can cause excessive coughing. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the throat and reduce swelling, making it easier to breathe and swallow. This can be especially helpful when we have a cold or flu, as it can help relieve congestion and coughs. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea has antiviral properties that can help fight off the underlying infection causing the cough.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relax.

Tannins, also known as English lavender, are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the airways, reducing inflammation and congestion that can cause coughing.
Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the airways, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs.
Alkylamides are good for coughing fits because they help to calm the airways, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of a cough.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for coughing fits
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that can help soothe a cough, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried lavender flowers. If using fresh flowers, rinse them gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine the lavender flowers with a cup of boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for about 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a light purple color and the aroma of lavender is noticeable. Strain the tea into a separate container using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the lavender flowers. You can then add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, before drinking the tea slowly to help calm your cough. It's recommended to drink this tea in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day, and to consult with a healthcare professional before using it to treat any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, can help with coughing fits. The tea is made from the root of the licorice plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe irritated throats and calm coughs. The root contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the throat and ease the irritation that can lead to coughing. When you drink licorice root tea, the compounds are absorbed into your body and can help to calm the muscles in your throat, reducing the urge to cough. Additionally, the tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off infections that can cause coughs. This can make licorice root tea a good natural remedy for coughs and colds, and it can be especially helpful for people who are sensitive to antibiotics or other medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, and calm the cough reflex, making it easier to breathe and relax.
Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm and soothe the airways, reducing coughing and making it easier to breathe.
Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to breathe.
Triterpenoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing coughing and promoting relaxation.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for coughing fits
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea, start by gathering dried lavender flowers from a trusted source. Begin by boiling fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop. Next, add one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to a teapot or infuser for every 8 ounces of water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the desired strength is reached. After steeping, carefully strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or the infuser itself. You can then add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use a small amount of honey as lavender can be quite potent. Once the tea has cooled slightly, it can be consumed to help alleviate coughing fits. It's essential to note that while lavender tea can provide relief, it is not a substitute for medical treatment. If your cough persists or worsens, consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and care.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for coughing fits because of its natural ingredients. The main ingredient, eucalyptol, is an oil that helps to ease congestion in the chest and sinuses. When you breathe in the steam from the tea, the eucalyptol loosens mucus and makes it easier to cough up. This helps to clear the airways and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce irritation. The tea is also known to have expectorant properties, which means it helps to bring up mucus and other debris from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Drinking eucalyptus globulus tea can help to provide quick relief from coughing fits and may also help to prevent them in the future.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve coughing.
Eucalyptol is good for coughing fits because it has natural decongestant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm the throat and reduce coughing.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for coughing fits
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that can help with coughing fits, start by gathering some fresh or dried lavender flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently pluck the petals from the stem and place them in a clean bowl. For a more potent tea, you can bruise the flowers slightly by pinching them between your fingers to release their oils. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the lavender flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the lavender petals. Once strained, your tea is ready to drink. To make it more comforting, you can add a drizzle of honey to sweeten the flavor. To help soothe a cough, drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally when the coughing fits are most intense.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, is a natural remedy for coughing fits. Thyme is a herb that has been used for centuries to soothe sore throats and calm coughs. The tea is made from the leaves of the thyme plant, which contain a compound called thymol. Thymol has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to fight off infections that can cause coughing. When you drink thyme tea, the thymol is absorbed into your body and helps to calm your cough and reduce inflammation in your throat. Thyme tea also helps to loosen and clear mucus from your lungs, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like bronchitis or pneumonia, where coughing can be a major symptom. Additionally, thyme tea has a soothing effect on the throat, making it a popular remedy for sore throats and coughs.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for coughing fits because it has natural antitussive properties that help to soothe and calm irritated throats, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing.

Carvacrol is good for coughing fits because it has strong antimicrobial properties that help to soothe the throat, reduce inflammation, and kill bacteria that can cause coughs and other respiratory problems.
Rosmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated airways and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated airways and reduce the severity of coughing.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for coughing fits
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering a handful of dried lavender flowers, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle and then pour it over the dried flowers in a teapot or cup. Allow the mixture to steep for about 5-7 minutes, depending on how strong you like your tea. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to let the flowers steep for a few minutes first to get the full benefits. Once the tea has steeped, strain it into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the flowers. Drink the tea hot or iced, whichever you prefer, to help calm your cough and soothe your throat. It's also a good idea to drink a cup of this tea before bedtime to help you relax and sleep better.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a popular remedy for coughs. Ginger contains a chemical called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe. When you're coughing, your throat and airways can become inflamed, leading to a persistent cough. Gingerol in ginger tea works to calm down this inflammation, providing relief from the discomfort and pain caused by a cough.  Ginger tea also has a soothing effect on the stomach, which can become upset when you're coughing. Coughing can irritate the stomach, leading to nausea and vomiting. Ginger helps to calm down stomach irritation, making it easier to manage coughs without feeling queasy.  Ginger tea is also easy to make at home, as it only requires fresh ginger and hot water. You can add honey or lemon to taste, making it a soothing and comforting drink when you're feeling unwell.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for coughing fits because they help to calm the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and clear out mucus.

Shogaols are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of a cough.
Polyphenols are good for coughing fits because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help calm and soothe the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.

Zingiberene is good for coughing fits because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, ease congestion, and soothe the respiratory system, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for coughing fits
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering dried lavender flowers or fresh ones if available. If using fresh lavender, dry them in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 1-2 hours or let them air dry for 2-3 days. Next, combine 1 teaspoon of dried lavender flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the lavender's medicinal properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the lavender flowers. You can add honey to taste, if desired, to help soothe your throat and calm your cough. Once the tea has cooled slightly, drink it while it's still warm to help ease your coughing fits and promote relaxation. It's essential to note that lavender tea is not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment of any underlying health issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus, also known as mullein, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with coughing fits. The leaves and flowers of this plant are used to make a tea that is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink Verbascum thapsus tea, the active compounds in it help to soothe and calm the throat and lungs, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause coughing. The tea also helps to loosen and clear out mucus and other debris that can get stuck in your airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the cough. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of the tea can help to calm down the muscles in your throat and chest, reducing the severity of coughing fits and making it easier to relax and get better.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the airways and reduce inflammation that can cause coughing.

Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing fits.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with coughing fits, you will need dried lavender flowers and a clean pot. First, boil some water in the pot and then add a teaspoon of dried lavender flowers for every 8 ounces of water. You can adjust the amount of flowers according to your preference, but this is a good starting point. Next, let the flowers steep in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and healing properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time is up, remove the flowers from the pot and let the tea cool down for a few minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a sieve or a strainer to remove any remaining lavender particles. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to drink the tea plain to get the full benefits. Finally, drink the tea hot or warm, ideally when you feel a coughing fit coming on, and you may find some relief from the discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a natural remedy for coughing fits. The menthol in peppermint tea helps to calm the throat and relax the muscles, making it easier to swallow. When you have a cough, the muscles in your throat become tight and irritated, causing you to cough even more. The menthol in peppermint tea works by numbing the throat and reducing inflammation, which helps to soothe the cough. Additionally, peppermint tea has antimicrobial properties that help to fight off the infection causing the cough. This means that the tea can help to reduce the severity of the cough and speed up the recovery process. Drinking peppermint tea can also help to loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out your airways.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol helps to relieve coughing fits by thinning mucus in the throat, making it easier to cough up and providing temporary pain relief.

Rosmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort caused by coughing.

Luteolin is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and airways, allowing for easier breathing and relief from coughing.

Menthone is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort associated with coughing.

Next, boil some water in a pot and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, add one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag, and place it in the pot with the cooled water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the lavender flowers to release their healing properties into the water. After the steeping time is up, carefully remove the tea infuser or muslin bag, and strain the tea into a cup to remove any loose lavender particles. You can now drink the tea hot or cold, sweetened with honey if needed, to help soothe your cough and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tea, also known as elderflower tea, is a natural remedy for coughing fits. The flowers of the elder tree contain special compounds that help to soothe and calm the throat. When you drink Sambucus nigra tea, these compounds work together to reduce inflammation in the throat and loosen mucus. This makes it easier for you to cough up the mucus and feel better. The tea also contains anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the airways and reduce the severity of coughing fits. Furthermore, Sambucus nigra tea has antiviral properties that help to fight off the underlying infection that's causing the cough. As a result, drinking Sambucus nigra tea can provide quick relief from coughing fits and help you feel more comfortable.

However, please note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, has been used for centuries to help soothe coughing fits. The active ingredients in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve congestion and calm the throat. When you drink rosemary tea, the warm liquid helps to loosen and clear mucus from your airways, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or flu and are experiencing a persistent cough. Additionally, rosemary's expectorant properties help bring up mucus and phlegm from your lungs, which can help break a coughing fit. Drinking rosemary tea can also help reduce inflammation in your throat, making it less irritated and sore. This can be especially helpful if you have a sore throat or are experiencing a cough that is making it hard to sleep.

Carnosic acid helps relieve coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and airways, reducing irritation and discomfort.

Next, boil water in a kettle and let it cool for a few minutes. Then, pour the cooled water over the lavender flowers in a teapot or a cup. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the lavender's medicinal properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into a separate container to remove the lavender flowers. Your Lavandula angustifolia tea is now ready to be consumed. To use it for coughing fits, drink 1-2 cups of the tea as needed, ideally when you feel a cough coming on, and try to relax and breathe deeply while sipping the tea. As the lavender's soothing properties take effect, you may find that your coughing fits become less frequent and less intense.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is good for coughing fits because it has natural soothing properties. The tea contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their calming effects on the body. These compounds help to relax the muscles in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and discomfort. When you drink Lavandula angustifolia tea, the anti-inflammatory properties help to ease the cough, making it less frequent and less severe. The tea also has a decongestant effect, which helps to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the calming effects of the tea can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can sometimes trigger or worsen coughing fits. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea can provide quick relief from coughing fits, helping you to feel more comfortable and relaxed.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to calm the airways and reduce coughing.
Linalyl acetate is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing inflammation and relieving the cough.
Caryophyllene oxide is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and calm the cough.

remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for coughing fits
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering dried lavender flowers. You can purchase these from a health food store or grow your own lavender plant. Next, boil a pot of water and remove it from the heat. Add one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag, and place it into the pot. Allow the lavender to steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, then carefully remove the tea infuser or muslin bag. Strain the tea into a cup to remove any loose lavender flowers. Finally, add honey to taste, as the sweetness can help soothe a sore throat. You can drink the tea as needed to help calm a cough, and it's especially helpful when consumed before bedtime to promote a restful night's sleep.

supplementary_best: For coughing fits, a blend of Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Verbascum thapsus is the most effective combination. Eucalyptus globulus has decongestant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a natural antiseptic and expectorant that helps bring up mucus and soothe the throat. Verbascum thapsus, or mullein, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm irritated tissues in the throat and chest, reducing coughing fits. This combination works well together because Eucalyptus globulus opens up airways, Thymus vulgaris helps bring up mucus, and Verbascum thapsus soothes irritated tissues, creating a powerful triple threat against coughing fits.
supplementary_avoid: If you have coughing fits, there are some herbal teas you should avoid.  One of these teas is made from Hyssopus officinalis (hyssop). While it's used to treat respiratory issues, it can irritate your throat and make coughing worse.  Another tea to avoid is made from Arctium lappa (burdock root). It can slow down your breathing and make it harder to cough up mucus, which can be a problem if you're trying to clear your lungs.  Tea made from Aconitum spp. (Aconitum) is also not recommended. This herb can cause problems with your heart rate and blood pressure, which can be dangerous if you have a cough.  Tea made from Valeriana officinalis (valerian root) can be a problem as well. It can slow down your breathing and make it harder to cough up mucus.  Finally, tea made from Asarum europaeum (wild ginger) should be avoided. It can irritate your stomach and make nausea worse, which can be a problem if you're already feeling unwell.  It's worth noting that if you have a cough, you should talk to a doctor before trying any herbal teas.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a thick, sweet liquid made by bees from the nectar of flowers. When you have a cough, eating a spoonful of honey can help soothe your throat and make the coughing less painful. This is because honey has antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation in your throat, and its thick texture helps coat the back of your throat, giving it a protective layer.  Gargling with warm salt water is another natural remedy that can help calm a cough. When you gargle, the salt water helps reduce swelling in your throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe. The warm water also loosens mucus in your throat, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or flu.  Mustard plasters, which are small packets of mustard powder wrapped in cloth, can also help ease a cough. When you apply a mustard plaster to your chest, the heat from the mustard helps loosen mucus in your lungs, making it easier to cough up. The mustard also has antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation in your lungs, making it easier to breathe.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience coughing fits often have other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. For example, bronchitis, an inflammation of the airways, can be relieved with thyme. This herb has antibacterial properties that help fight off infections and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe.  Asthma, a condition where airways become narrow and swell, can be managed with eucalyptus. The menthol in eucalyptus helps relax muscles and open airways, making it easier to breathe.  Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), a condition where airways become damaged and narrow, can be helped with licorice root. This herb helps reduce inflammation and protect the lungs from further damage, making it easier to breathe.  Pneumonia, an infection in the lungs, can be treated with yarrow. This herb helps reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making it easier to breathe and recover.  Sinus infections, which can cause coughing fits, can be relieved with goldenseal. This herb helps reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making it easier to breathe and recover.
